definition: A reframing where input costs rise faster than pricing power allows pass-through, compressing EBITDA for B-rated credits. A 25% commodity cost increase generates ~$51M incremental working capital for a $1B-revenue manufacturer, reducing FCF by 5-7%.
evidence: Working capital channel: 25% commodity increase → ~$51M incremental working capital for $1B-revenue manufacturer
5-7% FCF reduction from working capital amplifier
B-rated industrials facing the maturity wall are the slowest AI adopters
open_questions: How widespread is the margin squeeze across B-rated credits?
Does AI adoption timing mismatch systematically disadvantage the credits most exposed to the maturity wall?
